item.title: Framework Fallacy of Choice
item.description: Currently in my final semester of uni I am taking two courses in which I will use some sort of web framework It’s a no brainer that Fullstack Web Development will use a framework, I just didn’t know that it would be predetermined in this course we look at Vue and React I have some experience with Svelte which seems a bit like Vue’s promising younger sibling.

item.title: Game
item.description: A sample of some JS games When trying to learn frameworks I found making little games really helpful.

item.title: Svelte and Front End
item.description: Branching into the front end For most developers front end is at the very least a lightly explored skill.

item.title: Videos
item.description: Youtube Videos List of some videos, the main focus is tutorials / exhibitions of FOSS software right now I have videos that I did as part of a class project, I will add more and hopefully they will be less mundane!

item.title: Emacs Native Comp & Nix Package Manager
item.description: useful links for the Mac Os general *nix install What is native comp / GCC emacs ?

item.title: TA Java With Vscode Tutorial
item.description: Dr java vs vs code I know that Connamacher wants us using DrJava and I recommend this for most students.

item.title: Linux Opinion Piece
item.description: Windows / Linux / BSD / Mac Os windows I only used windows for a year before I started using linux.

item.title: Making the Best of the Worst
item.description: Covid and a year abroad I distinctly remember a professor writing on the whiteboard the number of sars cases in the US and the number in Cleveland.

item.title: Is emacs worth the strife?
item.description: Why use Emacs (Doom/ Spacemacs / Vanilla) Since I started studying Computer Science I have had to work with several different languages / frameworks and as a result have had to install a myriad of applications to handle each new challenge and or language.

item.title: Wrapping Up Training at Namsan
item.description: I was far from alone in terms of people who’s lives had been radically changed by covid.

item.title: New Website
item.description: I made this website using Hugo with the Soho theme

item.title: About
item.description: Cade Lueker aspiring software engineer and current student Education Case Western Reserve University Currently a CWRU student studying computer science Going for a B.

item.title: Grappling
item.description: Wrestling I began wrestling at 14 and decided it would be my only sport the last two years of high school (I was previously running track and playing football).
